description_long: This is a retractable utility knife, commonly known as a box cutter, featuring a slim, elongated body with a visible segmented metal blade extending from one end. The handle is primarily orange with black accents, likely made of plastic for durability and grip, and includes a black sliding mechanism on top for adjusting the blade length. The blade itself is straight, single-edged, and appears to be made of stainless steel, with visible score lines for snapping off dull segments. The sides of the handle are flat, while the top and bottom are slightly contoured for ergonomic handling. The overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative elements, focusing on functionality and safety. This type of knife is typically used for cutting cardboard, plastic, and other packaging materials in urban and industrial environments.
description: A slim, orange and black retractable utility knife with a segmented metal blade and a sliding mechanism for blade adjustment. The handle is contoured for grip and made of plastic, while the blade is straight and single-edged.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow tip of the extended blade and the orange and black handle.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the full length of the knife, the straight blade, and the sliding mechanism on top.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back end of the handle and the alignment of the sliding button.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, similar to view 1, showing the blade extension and handle contours.
attribute_handle_design: slim, contoured plastic handle with black grip accents and a sliding button
